description: Adversarial: a Class A artifact whose canonicalization is declared as a near-look-alike string ('RFC8785-JCS-v2') that is NOT the protocol-defined scheme MUST receive INVALID. v0.1.0 freezes canonicalization to the literal string 'RFC8785-JCS'. Look-alike scheme strings MUST NOT be honored.

input.claim: this artifact matches its declared origin digest under a corrupted canonicalization scheme name

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 §4 freezes canonicalization to the literal scheme 'RFC8785-JCS'. SPEC_LOCK_v0.1.md §2.1 freezes the scheme string under v0.1.0. A conformant implementation MUST reject any artifact declaring a non-literal scheme — including near-look-alikes such as 'RFC8785-JCS-v2', 'RFC8785-JCS/2', or 'rfc8785-jcs'. Mitigates threat A-1 (canonicalization corruption): an attacker who can convince a verifier to accept a look-alike scheme name controls the canonical bytes the digest is computed over and can therefore produce arbitrary 'verified' digests. Status MUST be INVALID. Exercises CS1 (artifact-declared scheme MUST match) and the v0.1.0 freeze rule that the scheme string is byte-literal.
